description:
An open-source alternative to Claude Cowork, powered by OpenCode

OpenWork is an extensible, open-source “Claude Work” style system for knowledge workers.
It’s a native desktop app that runs OpenCode under the hood, but presents it as a clean, guided workflow:
- pick a workspace
- start a run
- watch progress + plan updates
- approve permissions when needed
- reuse what works (templates + skills)
The goal: make “agentic work” feel like a product, not a terminal.
Highlights
- Makes session startup feel more reliable by preserving the empty-draft /session state and opening conversations at the latest messages.
- Improves responsiveness in long chats by triggering transcript windowing earlier and reducing composer reflow work.
Fixes & polish
- Fixes first-run behavior to create an initial chat and route non-media uploads into the inbox flow.
- Stabilizes sidebar/session selection behavior and removes top-flash/anchor jitter during load.
- Aligns share modal surfaces for more consistent theming and readability.
Packaging / ops
- Bumps OpenWork to 0.11.132 and updates package/version metadata across app, desktop, server, orchestrator, opencode-router, and lockfiles.
Compare:
https://github.com/different-ai/openwork/compare/v0.11.131...v0.11.132
